channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Milo's Global Journey: From Aussie Invention to International Favourite
item.description: Milo, the iconic chocolate malt powder, is often associated with Australia, yet its journey is surprisingly complex. While the beverage is now enjoyed in over 40 countries, and beloved in Southeast As...

item.title: Harmonize Opens Up About Past Debts and Current Financial Position
item.description: Tanzanian singer Harmonize, real name Rajab Abdul Kahali, has openly shared his financial journey with fans, revealing he faced significant debts in the past amounting to hundreds of millions of shill...

item.title: JMielle Wins Second Title as a Duo on 'Your Face Sounds Familiar,' Celebrates Bisdak Pride
item.description: JMielle, the dynamic duo on 'Your Face Sounds Familiar,' have once again captured the hearts of viewers by winning their second title on the popular reality show. Known for their unique performances a...

item.title: Benjamin Mkapa Hospital Seeks Support for Children's Stem Cell Treatment
item.description: Benjamin Mkapa Regional Referral Hospital in Dodoma is appealing for support, especially from the Miss World Tanzania 2026 contestants, to help cover the high costs of stem cell transplantation for ch...

item.title: Zanzibar Police Question Fiancé of Deceased US Influencer Ashlee Robinson Amid Ongoing Investigations
item.description: Police in Zanzibar are questioning the fiancé of American social media influencer Ashly Robinson, known as Ashlee Jenae, following her death on April 10, 2026. The couple had travelled to the islands ...

item.title: Enforcement Directorate remands I-PAC director Vinesh Chandel for 10 days in coal money-laundering probe
item.description: A Delhi court has remanded Indian Political Action Committee (I-PAC) director Vinesh Chandel to ten days in Enforcement Directorate (ED) custody in connection with a money-laundering case linked to an...

item.title: Capiz Tricycle Driver Wins P15 Million in Megalotto After 30 Years of Playing
item.description: A tricycle driver from Capiz finally won the P15 million jackpot in the Megalotto 6/45 draw held on March 25, 2026. The win came after three decades of persistent betting. The Philippine Charity Sweep...

item.title: Emil imposes suspension on Marcus in Episode 7 of Blood vs Duty
item.description: In Episode 7 of ABS-CBN’s primetime series *Blood vs Duty*, tensions rise as Emil, one of the lead characters portrayed by Richard Gutierrez, decides to suspend Marcus, played by Gerald Anderson. The ...

item.title: 33 Champions League Players Could Miss Semis If Booked in Quarter-Final Second Leg
item.description: As the second leg of the Champions League quarter-finals kicks off, 33 players are in danger of missing the semi-finals if they pick up a yellow card. UEFA rules dictate that any player who accumulate...

item.title: Amazon announces $16.3b acquisition of Globalstar to expand its satellite network capabilities
item.description: Amazon has confirmed its plan to acquire Globalstar, a US-based satellite operator, in a deal valued at roughly $US11.6 billion ($16.3 billion AUD). The move is intended to bolster Amazon’s own satell...

item.title: Taliss-IST Wins 18th Tanzania National Club Swimming Championships
item.description: Taliss-IST has once again shown their dominance in Tanzanian swimming by winning the 18th Tanzania National Club Swimming Championships. The club amassed a total of 448.50 points, led by a particularl...

item.title: L.A. City Council Panel Approves Proposal to Restrict E-Bikes on Recreational Trails
item.description: The Arts, Parks, Libraries, and Community Enrichment Committee of the Los Angeles City Council voted unanimously to ban electric bikes (e-bikes) from most recreational trails in the city. The proposal...

item.title: SAM Pharmaceutical Commissions New cGMP-Compliant Factory in Ogun as It Marks 55 Years of Operations
item.description: SAM Pharmaceutical Limited, one of Nigeria’s oldest pharmaceutical companies with over 55 years in human and veterinary medicine, has commissioned a brand new state-of-the-art manufacturing facility i...

item.title: UK Defence Chiefs Face Budget Cuts Amid Rising Global Threats
item.description: The UK Government is facing criticism over defence spending as military chiefs from the British Army, Royal Navy, and Royal Air Force are reportedly being asked to identify £3.5 billion in savings thi...

item.title: Abdilatif Abdalla Advocates for Kiswahili as Language of Instruction in Tanzanian Schools
item.description: The national debate over language of instruction in Tanzanian schools has resurfaced after renowned poet and writer Abdilatif Abdalla urged the government to follow Julius Nyerere's vision by making K...

item.title: Six Public Servants Face Court Over Alleged Theft and Money Laundering of Sh14 Billion
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Six public servants, including three employees from the Medical Stores Department (MSD) and three local government officers, have been arraigned at Kisutu Resident Magistrate’s Court o...

item.title: Search continues for J&K man who went missing during assault over cow transport
item.description: In Ramban district of Jammu and Kashmir, search operations continued on Tuesday for Tanveer Ahmad Chopan, a 25-year-old man who went missing after jumping into a stream to escape an assault while tran...

item.title: Prison Officer Shares Experiences and Advises Youth to Avoid Crime
item.description: In Nairobi, Chief Inspector Mary Kaburengo, a seasoned prison officer with over 36 years of service, ministered to former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju during his plea appearance at the Kibera Law Co...

item.title: Kelly+Partners CEO Brett Kelly faces margin calls after pledging shares worth $64 million as collateral for personal loans
item.description: Kelly+Partners chief executive Brett Kelly has received multiple margin calls after using more than 7 million shares in the company as collateral for personal borrowings. These shares, once valued at ...

item.title: Calls grow to set legal marriage age for girls at 18 in Tanzania
item.description: In Tanzania, there is a growing push to amend the Marriage Act of 1971 so that the minimum legal age of marriage for girls is set at 18 years. Right now, boys can legally marry at 18, but girls may ma...
